cards.back: The external world in which an intelligent agent interacts and perceives, including its physical and social surroundings
The ability of an intelligent agent to make decisions that maximize its goals and minimize its costs, taking into account its knowledge, beliefs, and preferences
The complexity and unpredictability of the environment in which an intelligent agent operates, requiring the agent to adapt and learn
The internal organization and composition of an intelligent agent, including its knowledge representation, reasoning mechanisms, and decision-making processes

The dynamic interaction between the intelligent agent and its environment, where the agent's actions affect the environment, and the environment's responses impact the agent's behavior and performance
